reasoning: The probability of OpenAI making a definitive, explicit announcement that they have "created an artificial general intelligence" in the remaining 7 weeks of 2025 is extremely low. While earlier in the year there was significant hype ("we know how to build AGI"), actual late-2025 reporting indicates that CEO Sam Altman is pivoting away from the term, calling AGI a "less useful term." The company has shifted toward a five-level capability framework instead. Furthermore, the structural realities finalized in late October 2025 make an impromptu declaration difficult: the newly restructured Microsoft-OpenAI deal introduces an "independent expert panel" to verify any AGI claims. Given this regulatory and legal friction, the shift in corporate rhetoric, and the lack of any credible leaks pointing to an imminent year-end declaration, the chances of a sudden "AGI achieved" announcement before December 31 are minimal. The market price of 5% in late October accurately reflects this skepticism, and incorporating the recent corporate restructuring leans the probability even lower.
